content.problemStatement:
These days, Chef is working on opening a restaurant. Obviously, Chef dislikes running the restaurant by himself. In order to avoid such a terrible thing, he decided to hire exactly MMM workers (numbered 111 through MMM) to help him.

The workers will choose some positive integer constants a1,a2,…,aMa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Ma1​,a2​,…,aM​. A worker with a constant aaa wants to be paid a fixed salary after each aaa days. Chef is very orderly, so he wants to choose a Chef-year — a period of xxx days, and a Chef-week — a period of yyy days, where xxx and yyy depend on the workers' constants in such a way that Chef can always pay each worker's salary at the end of a Chef-week and all the workers are paid their salaries at once at the end of a Chef-year.

Chef also wants a Chef-year to be as short as possible and a Chef-week to be as long as possible. It can be shown that these conditions are satisfied when xxx is the lowest common multiple of a1,a2,…,aMa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Ma1​,a2​,…,aM​ and yyy is the greatest common divisor of a1,a2,…,aMa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Ma1​,a2​,…,aM​. For a group of MMM workers with known a1,a2,…,aMa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Ma1​,a2​,…,aM​, Chef then defines the value of the group as xpf(y)x^p f(y)xpf(y)，where f(x)=∑i=0qfixq−if(x) = \sum_{i = 0}^q f_i x^{q-i}f(x)=∑i=0q​fi​xq−i is a polynomial of degree qqq.

Now, Chef wants to know the sum of values of the group of MMM workers over all possible sequences a1,a2,…,aMa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Ma1​,a2​,…,aM​ which meet the constraint x≤Nx \le Nx≤N. Since the answer may be extremely large, you should compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

**Note**: This problem has a stricter submission limit, each user may only submit this problem at most 50 times during the contest.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains four space-separated integers N, M, p, and q. The second line contains q+1 space-separated integers f0, f1, …, fq — the coefficients of the polynomial f.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the sum of values for all possible groups of workers modulo 10^9 + 7.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the sum of values for all possible groups of workers. We can use dynamic programming to calculate the sum of values for each possible group size from 1 to M. For each group size, we need to calculate the lowest common multiple (LCM) and the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the constants a1, a2, …, aM. We can use the formula for LCM and GCD to calculate these values. Then, we can use the polynomial f to calculate the value of the group. Finally, we need to sum up the values for all possible groups and take the result modulo 10^9 + 7.
solution.timeComplexity: O(M * N) — the time complexity is dominated by the calculation of the LCM and GCD for each group size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the dynamic programming table.
